topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up, bright and intrusive, right as I was trying to focus on quantum mechanics. It was Professor Albright. A picture of him, smiling awkwardly, captioned with a quote about intellectual curiosity. I stared at the screen, my jaw tightening. I had specifically blocked him on everything, yet here he was, actively broadcasting his life to the masses, including me. I slammed my laptop shut, the sound echoing sharply in the quiet library corner. Why? What was he trying to achieve by curating this digital presence? I rubbed my temples, the throbbing behind my eyes intensifying. This was just… too much.

[story 2]

I found the evidence tucked away in a forgotten folder on my phone—a recent post from my history teacher, detailing his weekend hike. A slow, burning heat rose up my neck. I reread the caption, the cheerful tone grating on my nerves. It felt like a deliberate invasion of my personal space, a casual disregard for the boundary between classroom and personal life. I paced my small dorm room, my steps quick and uneven. I wanted to confront him, to demand an explanation, but the thought of facing him felt monumental. I just wanted the digital noise to stop.

[story 3]

It started innocently enough—a shared class group chat, then a follow request. Now, Professor Davies was posting updates about his garden, complete with perfectly filtered sunlight. I watched the feed scroll by, a tight knot forming in my stomach. Every little detail felt amplified, scrutinized. I sighed, a long, drawn-out sound that seemed to carry all the frustration I was holding inside. I picked up my pen, but the ink felt heavy, useless. I just wanted the connection to vanish, to retreat back into the safe, unmonitored space of my own study materials.
